questions.prompt: Which protocol does DNSFilter SSO use?
questions.explanation: DNSFilter SSO is OIDC. It works with any IdP supporting the generic OIDC flow and has explicit guides for Entra ID, Okta, Google Workspace, and Active Directory.

questions.prompt: When SSO is enabled, the Account Owner can no longer log in with username and password.
questions.explanation: Owners always retain username/password login as the IdP-failure escape hatch. Super Admins can be configured to keep that fallback too. This is what unlocks the account during an IdP outage.
